text.title: spatial-ux-fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for spatial-ux-fundamentals as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Porting 2D screen layouts directly to spatial without remapping to field zones.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 2D patterns assume a fixed viewport. Spatial adds depth, reach, and gaze constraints that invalidate bottom-nav, top-bar, and modal-overlay patterns. Every element must be assigned a field zone explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Placing primary CTAs or form controls in the far field (3 m+) to "give the layout breathing room".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Far field is for ambient context only. Interactive controls at 3 m+ are unreachable by hand-ray for many users (arm length, reach angle) and untargetable by gaze at typical HMD resolutions.

failure-mode.description: Generating 3D assets from LLM text-to-3D for production use.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Text-to-3D quality, polygon count, UV mapping, material assignments, and licensing are all unsuitable for production spatial apps. Agents that generate geometry must not bypass the art-direction and budget-validation pipeline.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated USD or glTF with incorrect axis convention (Y-up vs. Z-up) shipped without validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Axis convention mismatches produce upside-down or sideways models in engine. Validate every generated asset through gltf-validator or usdview before import.
